title: hotato conversation test, schema conversation-test.v1
description: The PRIMARY user unit: a single file defining one testable conversation (Phase-1 design A). HONESTY INVARIANTS, structural: no `overall_score`, no weighting, no blended field anywhere -- success is a boolean over an explicit CLOSED vocabulary of named conditions, NEVER a merged percentage; deterministic and model-judged assertions live in SEPARATE lanes (assertions.deterministic vs assertions.rubric); the five report dimensions are a TAG on each assertion (dimension), never a scorer. Malformed docs are rejected by hotato.conversation_test.validate_conversation_test_doc (ValueError, exit 2) BEFORE any use, mirroring assert_.validate_assertions_doc.

scenario.description: Optional path/ref to a scenario file. In P1 the caller/behavior may inline instead of pointing at a scenario.

caller.description: The simulated caller: who they are, what they want, the ground-truth they hold, and how they behave. Optional -- a test may reference a scenario file instead.

facts.description: Known ground-truth the caller holds (used by entity/fact assertions later).

interrupt_at.description: Labelled points at which the caller barges in.

environment.description: Describes the test environment. All members optional.

assertions.description: Two SEPARATE lanes (the deterministic/model-judged honesty wall). `deterministic` = assert.v1-style assertions (pure, offline, no model). `rubric` = model-judged refs whose evaluator lands in Phase 3; in P1 a rubric assertion evaluates to INCONCLUSIVE.

repetitions.description: How many times the conversation is run; drives Reliability (pass^k), computed in Phase 2. Default 1.

inconclusive_policy.description: Reuses the Phase-0 assert.v1 field semantics: how an INCONCLUSIVE result (absent required input, never a failed judgment) gates. Default 'report'.

success.description: Success is a BOOLEAN over an explicit closed vocabulary of named conditions -- there is deliberately no weighting and no overall_score. The per-dimension scorecard groups by the `dimension` tag; it is never blended into one number.

required.description: A CLOSED vocabulary of named boolean conditions (validated). Success is their conjunction, never a score.

report_dimensions.description: Which of the five dimensions the report groups results into. A grouping key, never a weight.

assertion.description: One assertion in a lane. Its inner kind-specific fields are validated at RUN time (assert_ / a later slice's expanded kinds), not here -- this schema pins only the test-file envelope shape.

dimension.description: Optional TAG grouping this assertion's result into one of the five report dimensions. A tag, never a weight or a scorer.
